# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains method, architecture, evaluation, metric, and limitation evidence from an industrial case study on codifying expert domain knowledge into an LLM-based visualization agent for simulation-analysis workflows.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: claim-support
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper states that critical domain knowledge often resides with few experts, creating organizational bottlenecks in scalability and decision-making.

## Source Excerpt

"Critical domain knowledge typically resides with few experts, creating organizational bottlenecks in scalability and decision-making."

## Note

This is stated as problem context in the abstract.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The proposed framework augments an LLM with a request classifier, a RAG system for code generation, codified expert rules, and visualization design principles in one agent.

## Source Excerpt

"We propose a software engineering framework to capture human domain knowledge for engineering AI agents in simulation data visualization by augmenting a Large Language Model (LLM) with a request classifier, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system for code generation, codified expert rules, and visualization design principles unified in an agent..."

## Note

This is the paper’s explicit architecture-level description of how the system is constructed.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The evaluation covered five scenarios across multiple engineering domains and used 12 evaluators.

## Source Excerpt

"Evaluation across five scenarios spanning multiple engineering domains with 12 evaluators..."

## Note

This describes the stated evaluation scope in the abstract.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The proposed agent is reported to improve output quality by 206% relative to baseline.

## Source Excerpt

"...demonstrates 206% improvement in output quality..."

## Note

The abstract reports the improvement percentage without deriving it further here.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper reports mean output-quality scores of 2.60 for the proposed system and 0.85 for the baseline on a 0-3 scale.

## Source Excerpt

"...demonstrating 206% improvement in output quality (mean: 2.60 vs. 0.85 on 0-3 scale)..."

## Note

This is a direct numeric result reported in the contribution summary.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The proposed system achieved expert-level ratings with Mode=3 in all cases, while the baseline had Mode=0 in 4 of 5 scenarios.

## Source Excerpt

"...with our system achieving expert-level ratings (Mode=3) consistently versus baseline’s poor performance (Mode=0 in 4/5 scenarios)..."

## Note

The paper presents this as a comparative evaluation outcome.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The proposed system is reported to have lower code-quality variance than baseline, with human-evaluation standard deviations of 0.29-0.58 versus 0.39-1.11.

## Source Excerpt

"...and superior code quality with lower variance (SD: 0.29-0.58 vs. 0.39-1.11)."

## Note

This variance comparison is reported directly in the paper’s contribution summary.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Section 3.1.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The knowledge-extraction step used semi-structured interviews with two experts, each lasting about 60-90 minutes, recorded and transcribed.

## Source Excerpt

"We conducted semi-structured interviews with two domain experts..." and "Each interview lasted approximately 60-90 minutes and was conducted individually..." and "The interviews were screen-recorded with participant consent and subsequently transcribed for analysis."

## Note

This is the paper’s stated interview procedure for extracting expert knowledge.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: method
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Section 3.1.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The experts provided explicit rules that were codified into Python functions and integrated into the system architecture.

## Source Excerpt

"The experts directly provided explicit rules and guidelines during the interviews, which were captured and documented." and "These expert-provided rules were then systematically codified into Python functions and integrated into our system architecture..."

## Note

This is a direct statement of how extracted expert knowledge was operationalized.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: method
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Section 4.4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The framework separates implementation into executable code for explicit procedural rules and LLM augmentation for tacit design principles.

## Source Excerpt

"This insight led to our two-pronged implementation strategy: (1) executable code for algorithmic rules, and (2) LLM augmentation for contextual principles."

## Note

This is the paper’s explicit mechanism for handling different knowledge types.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: method
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Section 4.2.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For history plots, the visualization expert specified dashed lines for non-converged variables, solid lines for converged variables, and no more than two variables, objectives, or responses per display.

## Source Excerpt

"History plots should always display the best design for reference and limit displays to no more than 2 variables, objectives, or responses to maintain readability. Visual encoding should communicate convergence status through dashed lines for non-converged variables and solid lines for converged variables."

## Note

This is a concrete expert rule set for one plot type.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: method
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Section 3.1.4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The practical-effectiveness test used one non-expert mechanical engineer with 1 year of simulation experience, given a 30-minute orientation and no visualization training, while a visualization expert with 20 years of experience evaluated the generated plots.

## Source Excerpt

"We recruited a non-expert user from within the case company—a mechanical engineer with 1 year of experience in simulation domain but limited visualization expertise..." and "The participant was introduced to the system through a 30-minute orientation session covering basic functionality without visualization training." and "The non-expert user generated visualizations... which were then assessed by a visualization expert—a data visualization specialist with 20 years of experience in simulation data analysis..."

## Note

This evidence describes the human setup for the visualization-quality assessment.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: incident
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Section 6.2.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Initial testing found that standalone LLMs generated matplotlib and seaborn solutions incompatible with the Simulation Analysis software environment.

## Source Excerpt

"Initial testing revealed that standalone LLMs consistently generated Python-specific solutions using libraries such as matplotlib and seaborn, which are incompatible with the Simulation Analysis software environment."

## Note

This is a system limitation in the comparison setup and explains why the baseline used in evaluation was LLM+RAG rather than a standalone LLM.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-031
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: incident
citation: Ulan uulu et al., 2026, Section 8
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15153
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The paper states that external validity is limited because validation remained within simulation-based optimization contexts, and effectiveness in non-simulation domains was not tested.

## Source Excerpt

"Domain Specificity Validation spanned three physics domains but remained within simulation-based optimization contexts. Effectiveness in non-simulation domains (medical visualization, financial analytics) remains unvalidated, limiting external validity."

## Note

This caveat bounds how far the reported results can be generalized beyond the evaluated domain family.
---
